entities.name: AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of producing food and fiber to meet the needs of the global population

entities.name: GLOBAL POPULATION
entities.description: The increasing number of people worldwide driving the demand for food production

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: The unpredictability of global weather patterns that aggravates agricultural challenges

entities.name: PESTS/PATHOGENS
entities.description: Biological agents that cause disease or damage to crops, with dynamics influenced by climate change

entities.name: CROP IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: The application of breeding and genetic techniques to enhance crop characteristics

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to sufficient, affordable, and nutritious food

entities.name: CLIMATE-SMART CULTIVARS
entities.description: Crop varieties specifically bred to be resilient to climate change and sustainable

entities.name: FOOD PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of delivering food to the increasing global population

entities.name: CONVENTIONAL BREEDING FRAMEWORK
entities.description: The traditional approach to plant breeding that requires modernization to meet current demands

entities.name: GENOMICS
entities.description: The study of the complete set of DNA, providing tools to make plant breeding more precise and efficient

entities.name: PLANT BREEDING PROCEDURES
entities.description: The systematic methods used to develop new and improved plant varieties

entities.name: REFERENCE GENOME ASSEMBLIES
entities.description: High-quality genomic sequences used as a standard to identify breeding targets

entities.name: GERMPLASM SEQUENCING
entities.description: The sequencing of genetic material from diverse plant collections to identify breeding targets

entities.name: BREEDING TARGETS
entities.description: Specific genetic regions or traits identified for improvement to secure food supply

entities.name: PLANT GENOME SEQUENCING
entities.description: The process of determining the DNA sequence of plants to create genome resources

entities.name: GENOME RESOURCES
entities.description: The data and tools derived from genome sequencing used for trait discovery and manipulation

entities.name: GENE EDITING TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Technologies used to make precise alterations to the genome for trait discovery and manipulation

entities.name: TRAIT DISCOVERY
entities.description: The process of identifying the genetic basis of specific desirable traits

entities.name: TRAIT MANIPULATION
entities.description: The process of altering plant traits to achieve desired agricultural characteristics

entities.name: SPEED BREEDING
entities.description: A method used to accelerate the plant growth cycle to overcome breeding limitations

entities.name: GENOMIC SELECTION
entities.description: A breeding method using genome-wide markers to predict the performance of individuals

entities.name: HAPLOTYPE-BASED BREEDING
entities.description: A breeding approach based on the selection of specific combinations of alleles

entities.name: VARIETAL RELEASE
entities.description: The process of officially approving and releasing new crop varieties to farmers

entities.name: SEED DISTRIBUTION SYSTEMS
entities.description: The systems used to deliver improved seeds from research centers to farmers' fields

entities.name: GENETIC GAINS
entities.description: The measurable improvement in the genetic merit of a crop population

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE AGRICULTURE
entities.description: An integrated approach to farming that is environmentally sound and ensures long-term food production
